tweet: Let me be clear: The Justice in Policing Act does not defund the police, but rather holds law enforcement accountable while improving community services and programs that have been neglected for decades.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Most importantly, the Justice in Policing Act will help communities reimagine public safety by investing in mental health & homeless services, rehabilitation, & vital programs, while helping local police focus on work we really need them for, which is keeping our streets safe.

tweet: This Justice in Policing Act would create a system of accountability and transparency in law enforcement and eliminate harmful practices like chokeholds and no-knock warrants.

tweet: The moment @BetsyDeVosED took over, she delayed implementation of the 2016 rule--delays that were found illegal in court.   She’s attempted to implement a weaker borrower defense rule that ultimately undermines the original purpose of borrower defense--to help defrauded students.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: The 2016 Borrower Defense Rule, in part, included:  ✅Clear processes for how to file claims ✅Empower @usedgov to forgive loans quickly ✅Hold financially irresponsible for-profit schools accountable ✅Ban schools from misleading students to signing unfair arbitration agreements

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: After Corinthian’s collapse, the Obama admin updated the 1995 Rule b/c it “provided little detail on how borrowers could submit, and how @usedgov would adjudicate claims.”   In 2016, they issued the new defense rule--estimated to provide $17 billion of relief to students.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: The rule rarely had to be used until the major collapse of the predatory for-profit college, Corinthian College. Corinthian’s collapse left an estimated 350,000 students with worthless degrees and a mountain of student debt, the loans of which they couldn’t use. (3/9)

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: The original Borrower Defense Rule was implemented as a provision in the 1995 High Education Act to establish how student loan borrowers can receive forgiveness when defrauded by their higher ed. Institution. (2/9)

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: This Thursday, @EdLaborCmte will question @BetsyDeVosED on @usedgov’s inaction in upholding the Borrower Defense Rule, created to protect students from predatory #4profit schools & help defrauded students seek loan forgiveness. Before then, let’s set the record straight. THREAD:

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Since my first day in Congress, I have made it clear that I will fight any and every effort that threatens Nevadans’ access to quality, affordable health care, and I will not stop now.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: We are talking about the very real consequences people face if Congress refuses to act. Every American and most members of Congress agree that we need to do something about the skyrocketing cost of prescription drugs.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: That’s not a choice that any person, or family, should have to make in this country. That’s why we are fighting so hard to lower drug prices and protect the #AffordableCareAct from being torn apart.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: … and now they have to make the choice between getting their medicine and buying groceries, fixing their car, or paying rent on time. Last year, one in three Americans did not fill a prescription due to high prices.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Nearly every week, I hear from my constituents about how the price of their insulin, their heart medication, or their kid’s inhaler jumped 50, 75, and sometimes over 100 percent …

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: There are very few issues in our state and country that are as important as making health care more affordable and accessible - especially when it comes to lowering the cost of prescription drugs.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: I had a roundtable discussion w/ @RepHorsford @YvannaCancela @HCAN @BattleBornProg on what we need to do to reduce prescription drug prices and protect the #ACA.  https://t.co/1ScSi5tOY2

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Opioid abuse doesn’t care if you live in a red state or a blue state or a purple state, and addiction does not care about the D or R next to your name ... It’s immune to partisanship and socioeconomic status.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Well, I did something about it to get the ball rolling in Congress. The House passed my bill to increase funding for the Drug-Free Communities Program, which directly invests in local community efforts to combat opioid abuse.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: There are a number of ways to prevent and reduce opioid abuse, addiction, and death. The problem is that our communities--like southern Nevada and our rural communities—just don’t have the resources or the funding to implement the solutions that we know work.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: Opioid addiction is hitting our veteran population harder than any other, and it’s largely because of how our country has decided to treat them--by throwing prescription painkiller after prescription painkiller at them rather than giving them comprehensive medical treatment.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: At least one Nevadan loses their life to opioids every day, & that’s especially painful b/c we know it’s preventable. And for no group of people is that more true than for our veterans.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: We must treat opioid addiction like the public health crisis that it is. It is now the leading cause of accidental deaths in the US.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: We cannot allow politics to prevent progress in reforming our immigration system, and that’s why a bipartisan effort is the only way forward if we’re going to find a permanent solution to fixing our immigration system and helping those families at the border.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: That means more resources to process asylum claims more efficiently, more immigration judges & social workers, & investment in border security to stop cartels from taking advantage of our immigration system.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: If we’re going to fix our broken immigration system, we need a comprehensive solution that ensures both a fair pathway to legal citizenship for immigrants and resources for border security and enforcement.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: It is clear that we have not invested enough in the security of our border or the safety of those legally trying to immigrate to our country. I saw firsthand the challenges facing Customs and Border Protection Officers who don’t have the resources they need to do their jobs.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: I saw people seeking asylum in long lines, often up to 15 days, and were waiting desperately because there weren’t enough resources to process their claims efficiently.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: The people I met in custody put themselves at incredible risk to escape violence, aren’t sure what their future holds, & are being held in poor conditions. Yet, I could still see hope in their eyes. It made me understand more than ever we must come together to find a solution.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: I came away from my trip to the border acutely aware of how broken our immigration policy is. I also came away with a commitment to be part of the answer to fix our broken immigration system.

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: (Thread) My Visit to the Border in McAllen, TX, and the solutions we need to fix our immigration system:

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: UPDATE FROM THE #BORDER:  Ports of entry are so backed up. Clearly not putting enough resources toward processing asylum claims.  Instead of waiting in long lines, families risk crossing the Rio Grande here, 100s of ft wide. Here see a little girl's hat in mud near the riverbank.  https://t.co/1j9xlonJyd

name: Rep. Susie Lee
tweet: UPDATE FROM THE BORDER:  There's not enough resources here for border officers to process/care for people & families.  People legally seeking asylum waiting in long lines (up to 15 days) just to get through ports of entry.  The system is clearly broken.  More updates coming.
